zh.Azincourt, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-Franc: 法國豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯阿古特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Heer Johan II van Barbacon  Heer van Jeumont於1375年出生時，他的父親Jean I de Barbançon, Seigneur de Jeumont26歲，母親Jeanne Maubray du Chasteler25歲。他和Phillipotte de Werchin, Longueville en  Biez至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1415年10月25日在法國豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯阿古特享年40歲。

zh-hans.Azincourt, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-Franc: 法国豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯阿古特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Heer Johan II van Barbacon  Heer van Jeumont于1375年出生时，他的父亲Jean I de Barbançon, Seigneur de Jeumont26岁，母亲Jeanne Maubray du Chasteler25岁。他和Phillipotte de Werchin, Longueville en  Biez至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1415年10月25日在法国豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯阿古特享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Heer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Alfons, Alois, Ewald, Kurt, Monika, Otto.
German and Dutch: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the first element hari, heri ‘army’, as, for instance, Hermann . This surname is rare in the Netherlands.
Dutch: variant, without the definite article de ‘the’, of De Heer .

variants.name: Heerema
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Egbert.
West Frisian and Dutch (Groningen): patronymic from the personal name Heer , composed with the Frisian suffix -ma ‘(one of the) men of’.

variants.name: Heeringa
meanings: West Frisian: habitational name from any of several mansions or farms so called, for instance near Dongjum and Marssum, once inhabited by a certain Heere or Hero and his kin (see Heer 1). It is formed with the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a.

variants.name: Hehr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Elfriede, Erhart, Horst, Milbert.
German: variant of Heer 1 and 3, but in some instances a nickname either from hëher ‘jay’, or from Middle High German hēr ‘distinguished, proud’.

variants.name: Heeren
meanings: Dutch, North German, and East Frisian: patronymic from   Heer   1.

variants.name: Hermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Jurgen, Mathias, Gottlieb, Horst, Juergen, Klaus, Lorenz, Bernd, Dieter, Erwin.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Harman 5.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German name (see 1 above). Compare   Herman  .

variants.name: De Heer
meanings: Dutch: from heer ‘(feudal) lord, master’, with the definite article de ‘the’, probably applied as a nickname to someone who behaved in a lordly manner, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a feudal lord or the like. Compare Heer .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.

Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Johan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Older Scottish spelling of Joan , which was traditionally pronounced as two syllables.

variants.name: Jens
meanings: Mainly Danish: variant of   Johan .

variants.name: Jöns
meanings: Mainly Danish: variant of   Johan .

variants.name: Jan
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: Jon
meanings: Simplified spelling of   John   or a short form of   Jonathan .  The name is borne by the Canadian singer  Jon Vickers  (  b. 1926 ) and the American actor  Jon Voight  (  b. 1938 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

en.lifeSummary: When Heer Johan II van Barbacon  Heer van Jeumont was born in 1375, his father, Jean I de Barbançon, Seigneur de Jeumont, was 26 and his mother, Jeanne Maubray du Chasteler, was 25. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Phillipotte de Werchin, Longueville en  Biez. He died on 25 October 1415, in Azincourt, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heer Johan II van Barbacon  Heer van Jeumont est né en 1375, son père, Jean I de Barbançon, Seigneur de Jeumont, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Jeanne Maubray du Chasteler, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Phillipotte de Werchin, Longueville en  Biez. Il est décédé le 25 octobre 1415, à Azincourt, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Heer Johan II van Barbacon  Heer van Jeumont 1375 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean I de Barbançon, Seigneur de Jeumont 26 und seine Mutter Jeanne Maubray du Chasteler 25. Er hatte mit Phillipotte de Werchin, Longueville en  Biez mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 25. Oktober 1415 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Azincourt, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Heer Johan II van Barbacon  Heer van Jeumont è nato il nel 1375, il suo padre, Jean I de Barbançon, Seigneur de Jeumont, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Jeanne Maubray du Chasteler, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Phillipotte de Werchin, Longueville en  Biez. Morì il 25 ottobre 1415, nel Azincourt, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, all'età di 40 anni.

ja.Azincourt, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-Franc: フランスハウトス-デ-フランスプアス-デ-カレイスアズインコウルト
ja.lifeSummary: Heer Johan II van Barbacon  Heer van Jeumontが1375年に生まれた時，父Jean I de Barbançon, Seigneur de Jeumontは26歳，母Jeanne Maubray du Chastelerは25歳であった。彼はPhillipotte de Werchin, Longueville en  Biezとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1415年10月25日にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスプアス-デ-カレイスアズインコウルトで40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Heer Johan II van Barbacon  Heer van Jeumont(이)가 1375년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean I de Barbançon, Seigneur de Jeumont(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Jeanne Maubray du Chasteler(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Phillipotte de Werchin, Longueville en  Biez(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1415년 10월 25일 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 파스-데-캘레이 아지쿠르트에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Heer Johan II van Barbacon  Heer van Jeumont nasceu em 1375, seu pai, Jean I de Barbançon, Seigneur de Jeumont, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Jeanne Maubray du Chasteler, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Phillipotte de Werchin, Longueville en  Biez. Ele faleceu em 25 de outubro de 1415, em Azincourt, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, França, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heer Johan II van Barbacon  Heer van Jeumont родился в 1375 г., его отцу по имени Jean I de Barbançon, Seigneur de Jeumont было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Jeanne Maubray du Chasteler, 25. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Phillipotte de Werchin, Longueville en  Biez. Он умер 25 октября 1415 г. в местности азинкоурт, пас-де-калеи, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 40 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Heer Johan II van Barbacon  Heer van Jeumont nació en 1375, su papá, Jean I de Barbançon, Seigneur de Jeumont, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Jeanne Maubray du Chasteler, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Phillipotte de Werchin, Longueville en  Biez. Murió el 25 de octubre de 1415, en Azincourt, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 40 años.
